458	\section{Group-scheme structure}
459	\label{sec:fga_pic_group_structure}
460	
461	The Picard scheme \(\Pic_{C/k}\) inherits an abelian group structure from
462	the tensor product of invertible sheaves on \(C \times_k T\). The relative
463	Picard presheaf \(T \mapsto \Pic(C \times_k T)/H_T\) is already an abelian
464	group at every \(T\) (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
465	functorially in \(T\); this lifts to the \'etale sheafification, and then
466	to a group-scheme structure on the representing scheme by Yoneda.
467	
468	\begin{theorem}\leanok
469	[\(\Pic_{C/k}\) is a \(k\)-group scheme]
470	  \label{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}
471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure}
472	  \uses{thm:fga_pic_representability, thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
473	        def:pic_scheme, def:rel_pic_sharp}
474	
475	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Def.~df:Pfs (the relative
476	  % Picard functor takes values in abelian groups) and \S 4 surrounding
477	  % text (group-scheme structure on \(\IPic_{X/S}\) via tensor product);
478	  % arXiv:math/0504020, pp.~17, 26 (read from
479	  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318 + L2057-L2069).
480	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
481	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
482	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
483	  %      \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)
484	  %   Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
485	  %  topologies  by
486	  %      \(\)\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
487	  %      \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.\(\)
488	  %   \end{dfn}"
489	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
490	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Psch}
491	  %    If any of the four relative Picard functors of Definition~\ref{df:Pfs}
492	  %  is representable, then the representing scheme is called the {\it Picard
493	  %  scheme\/} and denoted by \(\IPic_{X/S}\).  Moreover, we say simply that the
494	  %  Picard scheme \(\IPic_{X/S}\) exists."
495	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Def.~2.2 +
496	  \S 4, Def.~4.1 (the Picard scheme).}
497	  Let \(\Pic_{C/k}\) be the Picard scheme of
498	  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}. Then \(\Pic_{C/k}\) is canonically an
499	  abelian group scheme over \(k\): the tensor product
500	  \([\mathcal{L}] + [\mathcal{M}] := [\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{M}]\)
501	  and inverse \(-[\mathcal{L}] := [\mathcal{L}^{-1}]\) on
502	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) (well defined because both operations
503	  preserve isomorphism classes and descend through the subgroup
504	  \(H_T = \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined})
505	  are natural in \(T\), hence by Yoneda are represented by morphisms
506	  \(m : \Pic_{C/k} \times_k \Pic_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}\),
507	  \(i : \Pic_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}\), and
508	  \(e : \Spec k \to \Pic_{C/k}\) (the trivial line bundle class) satisfying
509	  the abelian-group axioms. In particular, \(\Pic_{C/k}\) is a \(k\)-group
510	  scheme locally of finite type.
511	\end{theorem}
512	
513	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: not applicable --- Kleiman \S 2--4 do not isolate a
514	% separate proof block for the group-scheme structure: it is implicit in
515	% the fact that \(\Pic_{(X/S)\et}\) takes values in abelian groups (df:Pfs)
516	% and that representability transfers algebraic operations from set-valued
517	% functors to scheme morphisms by Yoneda. The structural restatement below
518	% records the standard transfer.
519	% NOTE (run-0008): this theorem is now PROVED in Lean, by direct Yoneda
520	% transport: with picSharp genuinely equal to the group-valued relative
521	% functor relPresheaf (def:rel_pic_sharp) with the forgetful functor
522	% applied, Mathlib's CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy applied to the
523	% representability witness `representable` yields the full COMMUTATIVE
524	% group-object structure (GrpObj + IsCommMonObj) on PicScheme C. The former
525	% ⟨sorry⟩-backed carrier pair (def:pic_scheme_group_object /
526	% def:inst_pic_scheme_group_object) is deleted. The Lean conclusion
527	% (CommGrpObj) is STRONGER than the abelian-group-scheme prose below asked
528	% for at the GrpObj level: commutativity comes with the same transport.
529	\begin{proof}
530	\leanok
531	  The relative Picard functor \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) of
532	  \cref{def:rel_pic_sharp} is abelian-group valued, with sum
533	  \([\mathcal{L}] + [\mathcal{M}] = [\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{M}]\),
534	  inverse \(-[\mathcal{L}] = [\mathcal{L}^{-1}]\), and unit
535	  \(0 = [\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}]\); these operations descend through
536	  the quotient by \(H_T = \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) because \(\pi_T^*\) is itself a
537	  group homomorphism (\cref{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}).
538	
539	  By \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}, \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) is
540	  represented by the scheme \(\Pic_{C/k}\). The Yoneda embedding is fully
541	  faithful and preserves limits, so a representing object of a
542	  group-valued presheaf is canonically a group object: the natural
543	  transformations
544	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \times \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$
545	  (multiplication), \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) (inverse),
546	  and the unit map \(h_{\Spec k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) pick out unique
547	  morphisms of representing schemes \(m\), \(i\), \(e\) as displayed, and
548	  the abelian-group axioms hold at the level of \(T\)-points functorially
549	  in \(T\), hence on the representing scheme. In Lean this transport is
550	  exactly \texttt{CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy} applied to the witness of
551	  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability} (transported across the
552	  \(\mathrm{Multiplicative}\) carrier identification); it produces the
553	  commutative-group-object structure directly. The local-finite-type
554	  property is part of \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}.
555	\end{proof}

589	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
590	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Psch}
591	  %    If any of the four relative Picard functors of Definition~\ref{df:Pfs}
592	  %  is representable, then the representing scheme is called the {\it Picard
593	  %  scheme\/} and denoted by \(\IPic_{X/S}\).  Moreover, we say simply that the
594	  %  Picard scheme \(\IPic_{X/S}\) exists."
595	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 4, Def.~4.1.}
596	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a field
597	  \(k\), with a \(k\)-rational point. The \emph{Picard scheme} \(\Pic_{C/k}\)
598	  is the \(k\)-scheme representing the relative Picard functor
599	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) (equivalently, by Kleiman \S 2 Thm.~2.5 under the
600	  rational point, its \'etale sheafification), supplied by
601	  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}, equipped with the abelian-group-scheme
602	  structure of \cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}. The scheme is separated,
603	  locally of finite type over \(k\), and a disjoint union of open
604	  quasi-projective \(k\)-subschemes.
605	\end{definition}
606	
607	\section{Lean encoding}
608	\label{sec:fga_pic_lean_encoding}
609	
610	The Lean target file is the new
611	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean}, sibling to
612	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}
613	(\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) and
614	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean}
615	(\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}). The blueprint declarations of
616	this chapter correspond to Lean targets as follows.
617	
618	\begin{itemize}
619	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme}
620	    (\cref{def:pic_scheme}) is a structural constant naming the scheme
621	    representing \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\); it is the scheme witness
622	    extracted from the \texttt{HasPicScheme} carrier
623	    (\cref{def:has_pic_scheme}) via \texttt{(HasPicScheme.has\_pic\_scheme
624	    C).choose}. The representability identification \texttt{representable}
625	    and the group-scheme structure are then layered on top of this bare
626	    scheme (so \cref{def:pic_scheme} depends only on \cref{def:has_pic_scheme},
627	    not on \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability} or
628	    \cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}).
629	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.picSharp}
630	    (\cref{def:pic_sharp_carrier}) is, after the run-0008 rewire, the REAL
631	    relative Picard functor: \texttt{PicSharp.relPresheaf} of
632	    \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} composed with the forgetful functor
633	    to \(\Set\) (values in \texttt{Type (u+1)}, since the line-bundle
634	    carrier is a quotient of large module sheaves;
635	    \texttt{Functor.RepresentableBy} is universe-polymorphic, so
636	    representability by a scheme remains well-formed).
637	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap}
638	    (\cref{lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence}) is the natural
639	    transformation \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) sending a
640	    relative effective divisor to its associated invertible sheaf.
641	    The construction uses the Hilbert / Quot scheme produced by
642	    \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}, restricts to the open subscheme of
643	    relative effective divisors via the Kleiman~\S 3 effective-divisor
644	    open criterion, and composes with the quotient map to
645	    \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\). (Still carrier-backed: the source
646	    \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) awaits the A.2.b engine.)
647	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient}
648	    (\cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient}) is the Altman--Kleiman
649	    descent of a flat-and-proper equivalence relation \(R \subseteq Z
650	    \times_k Z\) on a quasi-projective \(k\)-scheme \(Z\) to a
651	    quasi-projective quotient \(Q\), packaged in the form that consumes
652	    a \(\Functor.\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\)-witness for \(Z\) and produces
653	    one for the coequalising sheaf, with the conclusion supplied by the
654	    use-site hypothesis class \cref{def:has_smooth_proper_quotient}
655	    (see the run-0008 note after \cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient}).
656	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable}
657	    (\cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}) extracts the
658	    \(\Functor.\mathrm{RepresentableBy}\)-witness for
659	    \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) from the \texttt{PicSharpRepresentable} carrier,
660	    which is itself PROVED from \texttt{HasPicScheme} by
661	    \texttt{Exists.choose\_spec}; the four-step Kleiman assembly
662	    (Hilbert-polynomial stratification, \(m\)-regularity bound, Abel-map
663	    factorisation, smooth-proper quotient), preceded by the Kleiman \S 2
664	    Thm.~2.5 comparison, is the obligation of
665	    \cref{def:inst_has_pic_scheme}.
666	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure}
667	    (\cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}) is the \texttt{CommGrpObj} instance
668	    on \texttt{PicScheme} obtained by Yoneda transport
669	    (\texttt{CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy}) of the abelian-group structure
670	    on \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) — PROVED, sorry-free, after the run-0008
671	    rewire.
672	\end{itemize}
673	
674	No new core axiom is required: this chapter is an assembly chapter on
675	top of the QuotScheme engine, the relative-Picard \'etale sheafification,
676	and the line-bundle pullback machinery already in place. The single
677	large external input (the Altman--Kleiman theorem of effective
678	flat-and-proper equivalence relations on quasi-projective schemes) is
679	recorded as \cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient} and is itself a standard
680	result; if its Lean formalisation is not yet in Mathlib, the
681	\texttt{smoothProperQuotient} Lean declaration may be sorried and the
682	gap reported to the plan agent, as it is logically downstream of the
683	Hilbert-scheme construction of \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}.

693	are file-internal scaffolding for the public results; their substantive
694	mathematical content is the obligation analysed in

189	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
190	
191	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
192	`C/k` **with a `k`-rational point** is the `k`-scheme representing the
193	relative Picard functor `picSharp C` (which, under the rational point, agrees
194	with the étale-sheafified functor of Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main` by Kleiman §2
195	Thm 2.5). The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a
196	disjoint union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the
197	abelian-group-scheme structure is `groupSchemeStructure` below.
198	
199	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
200	
201	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
202	the relative Picard functor `picSharp C`. The single sorry-carrying site for
203	`PicScheme` is the `⟨sorry⟩` instance below, **conditional on
204	`[HasRationalPoint C]`** — without a section the plain relative functor is
205	not representable in general, so an unconditional instance would assert a
206	false statement. Consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
207	hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/
208	class HasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
209	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
210	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
211	  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
212	    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)
213	
214	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
215	integral curve `C/k`, encoded as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.
216	
217	Extracted (`Classical.choose`) from the `HasPicScheme` existence field, so it
218	carries the substantive identity "this scheme represents the relative Picard
219	functor `picSharp C`" through `representable` below — which, after the
220	run-0008 rewire, is a genuine natural bijection onto
221	`Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)`, not an opaque placeholder. -/
222	noncomputable def PicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
223	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
224	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
225	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
226	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
227	  (HasPicScheme.has_pic_scheme (C := C)).choose
228	
229	/-- Existence instance for `HasPicScheme` — **the single genuine FGA sorry of
230	this file**. Mathematical content: Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main` + Cor
231	`cor:algsch` (the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor of a smooth proper
232	geometrically integral curve is representable by a separated `k`-scheme
233	locally of finite type), combined with Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5 (under the
234	`k`-rational point of `[HasRationalPoint C]`, the plain relative functor
235	`picSharp C` coincides with the étale-sheafified one, so the same scheme
236	represents it).
237	
238	The conditionality on `[HasRationalPoint C]` is essential for truth: without
239	a section, `Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` need not even be a Zariski sheaf. -/
240	noncomputable instance instHasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
241	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
242	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
243	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasRationalPoint C] : HasPicScheme C :=
244	  ⟨sorry⟩
245	
246	namespace PicScheme
247	
248	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
249	
250	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
251	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` determines a
252	`T`-point of `Div_{C/k}`, and the dual ideal sheaf
253	`O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` represents a class in `Pic^♯_{C/k}(T)`.
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
256	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
257	
258	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/k}`.
259	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
260	`abelMap`. (The bare `Nonempty` statement is true — the pointwise-zero
261	natural transformation into the group-valued target witnesses it — but the
262	carrier stays opaque until the substantive Abel map arrives with the
263	Quot-scheme engine.) -/
264	class HasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
265	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
266	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
267	  has_abel_map : Nonempty (divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C)
268	
269	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/k}`, sending a relative
270	effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated invertible
271	sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
272	
273	Note the run-0008 rewire changed the TARGET to the real relative Picard
274	functor; the source `divFunctor C` is still an opaque carrier (see
275	`divFunctor`), so this natural transformation remains without defining
276	property until the Quot-scheme engine lands. Extracted from
277	`HasAbelMap.has_abel_map` via `Classical.choice`. -/
278	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
279	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
280	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
281	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasAbelMap C] :
282	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
283	  Classical.choice (HasAbelMap.has_abel_map (C := C))
284	
285	/-- Existence instance for `HasAbelMap`. This is the single sorry-carrying
286	site for `abelMap`. -/
287	noncomputable instance instHasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : HasAbelMap C :=
291	  ⟨sorry⟩

297	`Z` representable **by a quasi-projective scheme**, and the first projection
298	`R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper, the quotient `P` is representable.
299

512	C).hom` whenever the carrier class is in scope (as the identity-component
513	substrate requires). -/
514	instance {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
515	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
516	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C]
517	    [PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :
518	    LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom :=
519	  PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType.locallyOfFiniteType

855	\subsection{Carriers for the Picard scheme and its structure}
856	\label{subsec:fga_pic_scheme_carriers}

864	  \(X\) carrying a representability witness for the relative Picard functor
865	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) of \cref{def:pic_sharp_carrier}; in other words,
866	  that the Picard scheme \(\Pic_{C/k}\) exists. It is the existence carrier
867	  from which \cref{def:pic_scheme} extracts the representing object. Its
868	  (sole, sorry-carrying) instance \cref{def:inst_has_pic_scheme} is
869	  conditional on the \(k\)-rational point \cref{def:has_rational_point}.

882	  smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\) \emph{with a
883	  \(k\)-rational point} (\cref{def:has_rational_point}) --- run-0008: the
884	  conditionality is essential, since without a section the plain relative

887	  Step-0-through-Step-4 construction of \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}
888	  (Kleiman \S 2 Thm.~2.5 + \S 4 Thm.~th:main), gated on the Riemann--Roch
889	  substrate. See \cref{sec:fga_pic_sorry_closure_order}.

983	\section{Sorry-by-sorry closure order}
984	\label{sec:fga_pic_sorry_closure_order}
985	
986	% Run-0008 rewrite: the iter-199 seven-sorry carrier accounting is
987	% superseded by the picSharp rewire. The Lean file now carries THREE open
988	% sorries, all ⟨sorry⟩ instance bodies; every statement they assert is
989	% mathematically TRUE under the chapter's standing rational-point
990	% hypothesis. The historical per-sorry analyses (Sorries 1, 4, 6, 7 of the
991	% old numbering: instHasPicSharp, instHasSmoothProperQuotient,
992	% instPicSharpRepresentable, instPicSchemeGroupObject) are closed or
993	% deleted; see the git history of this chapter for the old text.
994	
995	The Lean target file
996	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean} carries,
997	after the run-0008 rewire, \textbf{four} open sorries, each isolated in a
998	single \(\langle\)sorry\(\rangle\) instance constructor of a
999	\texttt{Prop}-valued typeclass, so every extracted definition and theorem
1000	body is itself axiom-clean, and consumers that quantify over the
1001	typeclasses are kernel-clean. The four: \texttt{instHasPicScheme}
1002	(Sorry~A), \texttt{instHasDivFunctor} (Sorry~B), \texttt{instHasAbelMap}
1003	(Sorry~C), and \texttt{instPicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType}
1004	(\cref{def:inst_pic_scheme_lft}, part of the Sorry-A obligation ---
1005	Kleiman th:main(1)). The closed items of the former accounting: the
1006	relative Picard functor carrier is REAL (\cref{def:pic_sharp_carrier}),
1007	the representability identification is PROVED
1008	(\cref{def:inst_pic_sharp_representable}), the group-scheme structure is
1009	PROVED (\cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}), and the false global
1010	smooth-proper-quotient instance is DELETED
1011	(\cref{def:has_smooth_proper_quotient}).
1012	
1013	\subsection{Sorry A --- \texttt{instHasPicScheme} (the FGA theorem)}
1014	\label{subsec:sorry_has_pic_scheme}
1015	
1016	\paragraph{Location.} Instance
1017	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instHasPicScheme}
1018	(\cref{def:inst_has_pic_scheme}), conditional on
1019	\texttt{[HasRationalPoint C]}.
1020	
1021	\paragraph{Mathematical content.} The full FGA existence theorem: for a
1022	smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\) with a \(k\)-rational
1023	point, the relative Picard functor \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\)
1024	(\cref{def:pic_sharp_carrier}) is representable by a \(k\)-scheme. The
1025	proof is Step~0 (Kleiman \S 2, Thm.~2.5: the section makes the plain
1026	functor an \'etale sheaf) followed by Kleiman's four steps
1027	(\cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}): Hilbert-polynomial stratification,
1028	\(m\)-regularity bound, Abel-map factorisation through
1029	\(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\), and the smooth-proper quotient
1030	(\cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient}, whose use-site
1031	\cref{def:has_smooth_proper_quotient} instance for the Abel-map slice is
1032	part of this obligation).
1033	
1034	\paragraph{Blockers.} (i) the A.2.b Quot/Hilbert engine
1035	(\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}) for \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) and the Abel
1036	map (Sorries~B, C); (ii) the Route~C Riemann--Roch substrate
1037	(\(m\)-regularity / Serre vanishing / cohomology and base change for the
1038	stratification, cf.\ \cref{cor:flattening_stratification_curve} and
1039	\cref{lem:cech_flat_base_change}); (iii) Altman--Kleiman descent and
1040	EGA~IV~8.11.5, both absent from Mathlib. This is the load-bearing sorry
1041	of the picrep cone; it is NOT expected to close in a single session.
1042	
1043	\paragraph{Value of the run-0008 rewire despite the open sorry.} Because
1044	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) is now the real functor, the extracted
1045	\texttt{representable} witness has actual mathematical content for
1046	consumers: quantifying over \texttt{[HasPicScheme C]}, the natural
1047	bijection \((T \to \Pic_{C/k}) \simeq \Pic(C \times_k T)/\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\)
1048	is available as data. The downstream tangent-space identification
1049	(\texttt{Pic0.tangentSpaceIso}, chapter
1050	\texttt{Picard\_Pic0AbelianVariety}) attaches to exactly this bijection
1051	with \(T = \Spec k[\epsilon]\); it was impossible against the former
1052	opaque carrier.
1053	
1054	\subsection{Sorry B --- \texttt{instHasDivFunctor}}
1055	\label{subsec:sorry_has_div_functor}
1056	
1057	\paragraph{Location.} Instance
1058	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.instHasDivFunctor}
1059	(\cref{def:inst_has_div_functor}).
1060	
1061	\paragraph{Mathematical content.} The asserted \texttt{Nonempty} statement
1062	is trivially true (any functor witnesses it); the sorry is retained only
1063	so that the carrier \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\)
1064	(\cref{def:div_functor_carrier}) is flagged as awaiting its real
1065	definition: the subfunctor of the Hilbert functor
1066	\(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\) of relative effective Cartier
1067	divisors, representable by an open subscheme (Kleiman \S 3,
1068	Thm.~th:repDiv). Closes when \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme} commits its
1069	pin; until then the extracted carrier is OPAQUE and nothing may rely on
1070	its values.
1071	
1072	\subsection{Sorry C --- \texttt{instHasAbelMap}}
1073	\label{subsec:sorry_has_abel_map}
1074	
1075	\paragraph{Location.} Instance
1076	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.instHasAbelMap}
1077	(\cref{def:inst_has_abel_map}).
1078	
1079	\paragraph{Mathematical content.} The asserted \texttt{Nonempty} statement
1080	is true (even constructively: the pointwise-zero natural transformation
1081	into the group-valued target is natural), but the intended witness is the
1082	substantive Abel map \(D \mapsto [\mathcal O_{C \times_k T}(D)]\) of
1083	\cref{lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence}. Closes together with Sorry~B
1084	once the A.2.b engine lands (the run-0008 rewire already fixed the
1085	TARGET to the real relative functor, so only the source side is open).
1086	
1087	\subsection{Closure order}
1088	\label{subsec:fga_pic_closure_order_summary}
1089	
1090	Dispatch order: \(B \to C \to A\). Sorries B and C are gated on
1091	\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme} committing its Quot/Hilbert pin; Sorry~A
1092	additionally needs the Route~C Riemann--Roch substrate and the
1093	Altman--Kleiman/EGA descent inputs, and consumes B and C. Downstream
1094	Route~A consumers proceed under typeclass quantification
1095	(\texttt{[HasPicScheme C]}) and stay kernel-clean; the
1096	\texttt{[HasRationalPoint C]} hypothesis is supplied by the pointing
1097	\(P : \mathbf 1 \to C\) already present in the Jacobian statements.
1098	
1099	\section{Out of scope}
1100	\label{sec:fga_pic_out_of_scope}
1101	
1102	This chapter packages \emph{only} the representable Picard scheme
1103	\(\Pic_{C/k}\) as an assembly of sibling-chapter inputs, plus the
1104	group-scheme refinement. The following downstream constructions live in
1105	separate sibling chapters and are explicitly out of scope here:
1106	\begin{itemize}
1107	  \item \textbf{Identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)} (A.3) --- the open and
1108	    closed subgroup scheme of \(\Pic_{C/k}\) containing the unit and
1109	    consisting of those classes lying in the connected component of
1110	    \(\Pic_{C/k}\). Its dimension, smoothness, and abelian-variety
1111	    structure are the content of the A.3 chapter
1112	    \texttt{Picard\_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex} (planned), gated on this
1113	    chapter.
1114	  \item \textbf{Quot scheme representability} --- consumed by
1115	    \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability} via \texttt{thm:quot\_representable}
1116	    (\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}), not re-proved here.
1117	  \item \textbf{\'Etale sheafification of \(\Pic^\sharp\)} ---
1118	    \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} (A.1.c) constructs the relative
1119	    Picard presheaf; a topology-parametric sheafification
1120	    (\texttt{PicSharp.etSheaf}) exists there but, after the run-0008
1121	    route-(b) rewire, this chapter does NOT consume it: the standing
1122	    rational-point hypothesis makes the plain relative functor already an
1123	    \'etale sheaf (Kleiman \S 2, Thm.~2.5).
1124	  \item \textbf{Universal Poincar\'e sheaf} (Kleiman~\S 4, Ex.~4.3)
1125	    --- exists when \(\Pic_{C/k}\) represents \(\Pic_{C/k}\) itself and
1126	    \(C/k\) has a section. Under the chapter's standing rational-point
1127	    hypothesis both conditions in fact hold (Thm.~2.5 identifies the
1128	    plain functor with the represented sheaf), so the Poincar\'e sheaf
1129	    becomes available downstream --- but it is not constructed or
1130	    consumed in this chapter.
1131	  \item \textbf{Mumford's example} (Kleiman~\S 4, Eg.~4.14), counter-examples
1132	    to representability for non-integral geometric fibers --- not
1133	    relevant to a smooth proper geometrically integral curve.
1134	\end{itemize}
1135	
1136	\section{Internal-consistency check}
1137	\label{sec:fga_pic_consistency_check}
1138	
1139	The five declaration blocks form a connected \(\uses\) chain rooted in the
1140	sibling chapters \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback},
1141	\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}, and \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}:
1142	\begin{itemize}
1143	  \item \cref{lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence} uses
1144	    \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product} (A.1.b) and
1145	    \cref{thm:pullback_natural} (A.1.b).
1146	  \item \cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient} is a structural Archon-internal
1147	    statement; no external \texttt{\textbackslash uses} pointers.
1148	  \item \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability} uses
1149	    \cref{lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence},
1150	    \texttt{thm:quot\_representable} (A.2.b),
1151	    \texttt{def:rel\_pic\_sharp} (A.1.c),
1152	    \cref{def:has_rational_point},
1153	    \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} (A.1.b), and
1154	    \cref{lem:smooth_proper_quotient}.
1155	  \item \cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme} uses
1156	    \cref{def:pic_scheme}, \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability},
1157	    \texttt{def:rel\_pic\_sharp} (A.1.c), and
1158	    \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} (A.1.b).
1159	  \item \cref{def:pic_scheme} uses
1160	    \cref{def:has_pic_scheme} and \cref{def:inst_has_pic_scheme} only
1161	    (see the iter-283 cycle-breaking note at the definition).
1162	\end{itemize}
1163	The label \texttt{thm:quot\_representable} is a forward reference to the
1164	sibling chapter \texttt{Picard\_QuotScheme.tex};
1165	\texttt{def:rel\_pic\_sharp} is the (run-0008 repinned) relative Picard
1166	functor of \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}. If either changes label,
1167	the \texttt{\textbackslash uses} pointers should be regenerated across
1168	the chapters in lockstep.

data.content:
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
210	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
211	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
212	  sorry
213	
214	end Scheme
215	
216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
228	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
229	
230	namespace Scheme
231	
232	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
233	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
234	
235	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
236	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
237	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
238	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
239	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
240	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
241

252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,

258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.

277	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
283	
284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).

300	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
301	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
302	
303	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
304	
305	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
306	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
307	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
308	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
309	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
310	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
311	
312	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
313	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
314	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
315	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
316	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
317	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
318	
319	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
320	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
321	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
322	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
323	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
324	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
325	body is a typed `sorry`. -/

327	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
328	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
329	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by

346	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure

351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)

374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`

387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.

404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the

409	noncomputable def canonicalBaseChangeMap
410	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
411	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
412	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) :
413	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g ⟶
414	      Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f' :=
415	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f')
418	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g) ≪≫
419	        Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ≪≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)

433	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F), U) =
434	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F, f' ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

478	    Γ(N, V) →ₗ[Γ(X, V)]

480	  (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).unit.app N).val.app (.op V)).hom

483	base map for the affine-open section formula.

488	map

491	`(g.appLE V U e).hom : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, U)`.

494	`Γ((pushforward g).obj ((pullback g).obj N), V)`, is definitionally equal
495	to `Γ((pullback g).obj N, g ⁻¹ᵁ V)` by `pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl), which is
496	what makes the composition with the presheaf restriction typecheck.

500	(definitional from `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appLE`): linearity in the

689	identified with the section over `U` itself. Substantive content combines
690	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isoTop` (Mathlib HAS) with the `hU.isoSpec`

694	`Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (Mathlib's identification of the
695	`pullback` functor with the `restrict` functor along an open immersion;
696	applicable since `hU.fromSpec` carries `IsOpenImmersion` via
697	`IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`) with the definitional
698	`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj` (sections of `N.restrict f` over `V` equal
699	sections of `N` over `f ''ᵁ V`, by `rfl`) and the propositional
700	`Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` + `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`
701	to identify `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`. -/
702	private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
703	    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
704	    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
705	    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
706	    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
707	    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
708	    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
709	    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
710	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
711	    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
712	      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
713	    Nonempty (Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
714	      Γ(N, U)) := by
715	  letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
716	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
717	  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
718	    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
719	  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
720	  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
721	  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
722	  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
723	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
724	  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
725	  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
726	    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
727	  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
728	  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
729	  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
730	  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
731	  -- Define the additive equivalence.
732	  let toFun : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) → Γ(N, U) := fun x =>
733	    (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x)
734	  let invFun : Γ(N, U) → Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) := fun y =>
735	    (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y)
736	  have left_inv : Function.LeftInverse invFun toFun := by
737	    intro x
738	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
739	      eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
740	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
741	      ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app]
742	  have right_inv : Function.RightInverse invFun toFun := by
743	    intro y
744	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app,
745	      isoSheaf.inv_hom_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
746	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
747	      ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id,
748	      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
749	  have map_add' : ∀ x y, toFun (x + y) = toFun x + toFun y := by
750	    intro x y
751	    change (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op))
752	      ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
753	      _ + _
754	    rw [show ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
755	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) x +
756	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) y from
757	      AddMonoidHom.map_add _ _ _]

1333	map `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶ (pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)`
1334	is an isomorphism.
1335	
1336	This is the substantive content of Stacks 02KH(ii) (the `i = 0` form), and
1337	splits cleanly into two named substantive Mathlib gaps:
1338	* `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` — the affine case
1339	  via algebraic flat base change `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` (Stacks 00H8 /
1340	  02KE);

1342	  affine opens to arbitrary opens via Mayer-Vietoris on the quasi-separated
1343	  morphism `f`.
1344	
1345	The body of this theorem composes the two helpers cleanly; the substantive
1346	content has been factored into the helper bodies. -/
1347	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}

255	end Scheme

257	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
258	
259	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
260	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
261	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
262	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
263	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
264	
265	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
266	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
267	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
268	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
269	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
270	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
271	
272	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
273	
274	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
275	
276	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
277	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
278	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
279	
280	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
281	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
282	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
283	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
284	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
285	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
286	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
287	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
288	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
289	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
290	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
291	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
292	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
293	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
294	  sorry
295	
296	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
297	
298	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
299	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
300	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
301	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
302	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
303	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
304	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
305	
306	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
307	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
308	
309	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
310	
311	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
312	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
313	subschemes such that
314	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
315	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
316	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
317	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
318	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
319	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
320	  scope).
321	
322	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
323	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
324	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
325	
326	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
327	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
328	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
329	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
330	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
331	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
332	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
333	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
334	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
335	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
336	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
337	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
338	  sorry
339	
340	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
341	case opening]
342	
343	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
344	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
345	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
346	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
347	
348	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
349	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
350	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
351	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
352	Noetherianity).
353	
354	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
355	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
356	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
357	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
358	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
359	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
360	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
361	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
362	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
363	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
365	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
366	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
367	  sorry
368	
369	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
370	
371	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
372	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
373	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
374	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
375	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
376	
377	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
378	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
379	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
380	
381	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
382	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
383	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
384	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
385	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
386	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
387	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
388	iter-177+ work. -/
389	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
390	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
391	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
392	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
393	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
394	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
395	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
396	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
397	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
398	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
399	  sorry

401	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
402	
403	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
404	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
405	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
406	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
407	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
408	
409	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
410	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
411	
412	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
413	Stacks 052H].
414	
415	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
416	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
417	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
418	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
419	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
420	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
421	
422	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
423	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
424	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
425	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
426	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
427	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
428	type-clarity.
429	
430	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
431	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
432	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
433	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
434	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
435	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
436	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
437	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
438	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
439	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
440	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
441	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
442	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
443	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
444	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
445	  sorry

447	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
448	
449	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
450	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
451	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
452	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
453	
454	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
455	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
456	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
457	Nitsure's part (ii).
458	
459	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
460	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
461	
462	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
463	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
464	
465	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
466	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
467	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
468	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
469	
470	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
471	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
472	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
473	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
474	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
475	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
476	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
477	available. -/
478	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
479	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
480	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
481	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
482	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
483	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
484	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
485	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
486	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
487	  sorry

489	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
490	
491	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
492	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
493	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
494	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
495	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
496	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
497	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
498	explicitly. -/
499	
500	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
501	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
502	
503	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
504	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
505	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
506	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
507	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
508	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
509	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
510	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
511	
512	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
513	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
514	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
515	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
516	typed `sorry`. -/
517	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
518	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
519	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
520	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
521	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
522	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
523	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
524	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
525	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
526	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
527	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
528	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
529	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by

=== pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde + pullback_isQuasicoherent (CechHDIU) ===
`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` (proved via `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover).
Project-local; blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`. -/
theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent (V : X.Opens) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F).IsQuasicoherent :=
  isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens V F hF

/-- **Altitude 1 of the bridge: `(V.ι)^* F` pushed to `Spec Γ(X,V)` is `tilde N`** (Stacks 01I8).
For a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules` and an affine open `V` of `X`, the restriction `(V.ι)^* F`,
pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso `isoSpec : V ≅ Spec Γ(X, V)`, is canonically isomorphic to
the `tilde` of its module of global sections `N = Γ(Spec Γ(X,V), -)`.  The pullback is quasi-coherent
(`pullback_isQuasicoherent`) and quasi-coherence is preserved by the iso-pushforward
(`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the unconditional affine structure theorem 01I8
(`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, via the live instance `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 1). -/
noncomputable def pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≅
      tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F))) :=
  haveI : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)).IsQuasicoherent :=
    pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh hV.isoSpec ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)
      (pullback_isQuasicoherent V F hF)
  qcoh_iso_tilde_sections _

/-- **Altitude 2 of the bridge: `f_*(p_* p^* F) ≅ (Spec φ)_* (tilde N)`** (Stacks 01I8, assembled
pushed-forward level).  Over the affine base `S = Spec R`, with `V` an affine open of `X` and
`φ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f) : R ⟶ Γ(X, V)` the ring map presenting the composite
`isoSpec.inv ≫ V.ι ≫ f = fromSpec ≫ f` as `Spec.map φ`, the pushed-forward push–pull object
`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))` is canonically isomorphic to
`(pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde N)`.

The construction: collapse `f_* ∘ (V.ι)_*` to `(V.ι ≫ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`; rewrite
`V.ι ≫ f = isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map φ` (from `Spec.map_preimage` and `isoSpec_inv_ι`) by
`pushforwardCongr`; split off `(Spec.map φ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again (leaving the altitude-1
domain `(isoSpec.hom)_* ((V.ι)^* F)`); then push altitude 1 (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`) through
`(Spec.map φ)_*`.  The right-hand side is exactly the form consumed by the brick
`cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 2). -/
noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :

=== qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation ===
noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation (F : (Spec R).Modules)
    (P : F.Presentation) : F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm

/-- The hom of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the inverse of the tilde–Γ counit. -/
@[simp]
lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_hom (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).hom = inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
  rfl

/-- The inverse of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F`. -/
@[simp]
lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_inv (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).inv = F.fromTildeΓ :=
  rfl

/-! ### Reduction to global generation (Stacks 01I8, steps 2–3)

The unconditional quasi-coherent instance `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` is, by the
three-step 01I8 decomposition (`rem:o1i8_decomposition`), reduced to producing two *global*
generating families: one for `F` itself and one for the kernel of the resulting epimorphism.  The
declarations below formalise steps (2)–(3) — assembling those two families into a global
presentation and feeding it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` — turning what were
prose steps in the Handoff into axiom-clean Lean.  The single remaining mathematical input is the
affine global-generation theorem (step (1)), which supplies the two `GeneratingSections`.
-/

/-- A finite-free / free `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module is quasi-coherent: it is the tilde of `ι →₀ R`
(`tildeFinsupp`), and quasi-coherence is closed under isomorphism.  Project-local supplement; used
to recognise the kernel-side coefficient sheaf of the 01I8 presentation route as quasi-coherent. -/
instance free_isQuasicoherent (ι : Type u) :
    (SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) ι).IsQuasicoherent :=
  (SheafOfModules.isQuasicoherent.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).prop_of_iso
    (tildeFinsupp (R := R) ι) inferInstance

/-- **01I8 steps (2)–(3), packaged.**  If an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is globally generated
(`σ : F.GeneratingSections`, a global epimorphism `free σ.I ⟶ F`) and the kernel of that
epimorphism is itself globally generated (`τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections`), then the
tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` is an isomorphism.  This bundles the two generating families
into a global `F.Presentation` and feeds it to Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`; it is
the formal content of steps (2)–(3) of the 01I8 decomposition.  The single remaining mathematical
input is the affine global-generation theorem (step (1)) producing `σ` and `τ` for a quasi-coherent
`F`.  Project-local because it repackages the Mathlib presentation criterion in the
two-generating-families form the 01I8 route consumes. -/
lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
    IsIso F.fromTildeΓ := by
  have P : F.Presentation := { generators := σ, relations := τ }
  exact isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P

/-- **Affine structure theorem from global generation (Stacks 01HV/01I8).**  An `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module
`F` that is globally generated (`σ`) together with a globally generated kernel of the generating
epimorphism (`τ`) is isomorphic to the sheaf associated with its module of global sections
`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  Discharges the `IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` hypothesis of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections`.  Project-local for the same packaging reason; once the
affine global-generation theorem supplies `σ`/`τ` for quasi-coherent `F` the named
`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` upgrades to the unconditional statement. -/
noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections (F : (Spec R).Modules)
    (σ : F.GeneratingSections) (τ : (kernel σ.π).GeneratingSections) :
    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_genSections F σ τ
  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm

/-! ### Route P, step 0 — finite trivialising standard cover

The pure-topology brick of the global-generation route: any open cover of an affine
`Spec R` refines to a *finite* cover by basic opens, each contained in a cover member,
with the defining elements generating the unit ideal.  This is the common prerequisite
of the localisation-of-sections and global-generation steps. -/

/-- **Finite basic-open refinement of a cover of `Spec R` (Stacks 01I8, topology brick).**
Given a family of opens `U : ι → (Spec R).Opens` covering the whole space
(`⨆ i, U i = ⊤`), there are finitely many elements `f : Fin n → R` and indices
`φ : Fin n → ι` such that each basic open `D(f j)` lies inside `U (φ j)` and the `f j`
generate the unit ideal (equivalently the `D(f j)` already cover `Spec R`).  Project-local

| QuotScheme:275 | `Grassmannian.representable` | `thm:grassmannian_representable`, Nitsure §1 | **A** | glue `C(r,d)` charts along Plücker cocycle; deep. |
| QuotScheme:330 | `QuotScheme` | `thm:quot_representable`, Nitsure §5 / FGA | **A** | four-step boundedness→Grassmannian→flattening→valuative; the headline. |
| QuotScheme:596 | `pullback_tildeIso` | Stacks 01HQ / 0BJ8 | **A/hard-B** | "pullback of tilde = tilde of base change"; genuine Mathlib gap, Σ-pair section identity. |
| QuotScheme:620 | `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` | Stacks 01XJ | **A/hard-B** | qcqs pushforward preserves qcoh; Mathlib lacks it, project only has iso-pushforward + unfinished Čech qcqs machinery. |
| QuotScheme:676 | `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` | Stacks 01I8 | **B (risky)** | sheaf iso already proved in project (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, `pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`); residual is the Σ-pair section transport, project-flagged `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL`. |
| QuotScheme:1065 | `pullback_app_isoTensor` (…sectionLinearEquiv Stages 2–6) | Stacks 01HQ / 02KE | **A** | multi-stage section formula; depends on 596 + 676. |
| QuotScheme:1227 | `…isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase` | Stacks 02KE / 00H8 | **A** | Beck–Chevalley intertwining; depends on 1065 + 596. |
| QuotScheme:1278 | `…isIso_of_isAffineOpen` | Stacks 02KE / 00H8 | **A** | base-side Mayer–Vietoris descent to affine base, not built. |
| QuotScheme:1328 | `…isIso_of_affineCover` | Stacks 02KH(ii) | **A** | needs `Sheaf.Hom.isIso_on_basis` MV descent, not in scope. |
| FlatStrat:294 | `genericFlatness` | `thm:generic_flatness`, Nitsure §4 | **A** | Noether normalisation + generic freeness. |
| FlatStrat:338 | `flatLocusStratification` | `lem:flat_locus_open`, Nitsure §4 (Lem 5) | **A** | Fitting-ideal rank strata. |
| FlatStrat:367 | `flatLocusReduction` | `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`, Nitsure §4 (Lem 6) | **A** | Noetherian induction on closed complement. |
| FlatStrat:399 | `flatLocusAssembly` | `lem:noetherian_induction_strata`, Nitsure §4 (Lem 7) | **A** | direct images `E_i=π_*𝓕(N+i)` + Hilbert-poly indexing. |
| FlatStrat:445 | `flatteningStratification` | `thm:flattening_stratification_exists` / Stacks 052H | **A** | assembles 367+399; the headline. |
| FlatStrat:487 | `flatteningStratification_universal` | Nitsure §4(ii) / 052H | **A** | Yoneda universal property of the strata. |
| FlatStrat:530 | `flatteningStratification.ofCurve` | Nitsure §4 corollary (Route-A) | **C→B** | pure reduction: `exact flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F`; `IsProper (pullback.snd …)` auto from Mathlib. Closable now. |

## Ranked shortlist (most valuable + achievable)

1. **`flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (FlatStrat:530).** *Achievable:* its conclusion is literally `flatteningStratification`'s conclusion with `π := pullback.snd C.hom T.hom`, `S := T.left`; Mathlib's `instance … : IsProper (Limits.pullback.snd f g)` (Proper.lean:78) supplies properness from `[IsProper C.hom]` automatically, so `exact flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F` should close it. This is a *correct* proof (delegation to the main theorem), not a placeholder witness — it removes a leaf sorry and wires the Route-A consumer to the theorem. Low risk. Caveat: it rests on the still-sorry 445, so it does not reduce axiom-debt, only leaf count.

2. **`instHasAbelMap` (FGAPicRep:291).** *Achievable:* `picSharp C = PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` is group-valued, so the zero natural transformation `divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` (components `fun _ => 0`, naturality by `map_zero` of the group-hom restrictions) inhabits the required `Nonempty`. Moderate NatTrans plumbing; genuine (if placeholder) Abel-map carrier.

3. **`instHasDivFunctor` (FGAPicRep:185).** *Achievable:* `⟨(CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj (ULift Unit)⟩` inhabits `Nonempty (…ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1))`. Guaranteed one-liner; lowest mathematical value (the functor is opaque by design either way).

Honourable mention (highest math value, **not** a safe one-session close): **`tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (676, Stacks 01I8).** The project already owns the unconditional sheaf-level result — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, 0 sorries) and `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`. But the QuotScheme helper demands a specific `Σ`-pair section-level identity (`iso.inv ∘ tilde.toOpen = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap`), which is exactly the "01HQ transport / section-vs-tensor" gap the project flags as un-closed. Real content is there; matching the section identity is likely more than one session.

## Verdict

No headline theorem is realistically closable in one session. `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`/`QuotScheme`, `Grassmannian`/`Grassmannian.representable`, `genericFlatness`, and `flatteningStratification`(+`_universal`) each require machinery Mathlib v4.31 simply does not have — relative ℙⁿ_S, coherent-sheaf χ / Snapper polynomiality, Castelnuovo–Mumford boundedness, Fitting-ideal rank strata, generic freeness — and the project's own supporting engine (the Čech flat-base-change route `cech_flatBaseChange`, Stacks 02KH, in `Cohomology/`) is itself unfinished: `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` still carries 41 sorries and `FlatBaseChange`/`FlatBaseChangeGlobal` carry 2/1, so the QuotScheme §5 base-change chain (596/620/1065/1227/1278/1328) cannot yet be discharged from existing infrastructure. The genuinely valuable tilde/base-change helpers all bottom out in the same un-closed "01HQ / 02KE section-vs-tensor transport" gap. Realistic one-session work is therefore limited to (i) the correct leaf reduction `ofCurve → flatteningStratification` (530), and (ii) the FGA `Nonempty` carriers 185 and 291 (const functor / zero natural transformation) — sorry-eliminations of modest but real value, none of which advances a headline result.

# Recommendation — run 0010, focus `tasks=T12` (`AJC.picrep`, FGA Picard representability)

_Opening Ground; no prior Horizon diff. Tree confirmed green (`lake build`, 8642 jobs). Grounded suggestions — self-scope and override if you see better._

- **T12 has no headline single-session win; be honest about the ceiling.** The 23 in-scope sorries (`FGAPicRepresentability` 4, `QuotScheme` 12, `FlatteningStratification` 7; Grassmannian/RelPicFunctor sorry-free) are almost all deep Nitsure §4/§5 constructions needing Mathlib-absent machinery (relative $\mathbb P^n_S$, coherent-sheaf $\chi$/Hilbert polynomial, CM boundedness, Fitting-ideal strata, generic freeness, Altman–Kleiman descent). Do **not** try to close `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotScheme`, `Grassmannian.representable`, `genericFlatness`, or `flatteningStratification` — each is multi-session. Full triage is a roadmap comment on `AJC.picrep`.

- **Safe, correct leaf to bank: `flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (`FlatteningStratification.lean` L530).** It is literally the main theorem specialised to `π := pullback.snd C.hom T.hom`; `IsProper (pullback.snd …)` is a Mathlib instance, so `exact flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F` should close it. Correct delegation, wires the Route-A consumer. Caveat: it rests on the still-open L445, so it reduces leaf count, not axiom-debt — verify with `#print axioms` and don't over-claim.

- **Highest real-math-value target (stretch, likely >1 session): the Stacks-01I8 tilde helper `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean` L676).** The substrate exists sorry-free — `Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) and `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`. The residual is the Σ-pair section-vs-tensor identity (`iso.inv ∘ tilde.toOpen = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap`). If you take it, time-box it and fall back to `ofCurve` if the section identity resists.

- **Do NOT vacuously close `instHasDivFunctor` (L185) / `instHasAbelMap` (L291).** Their `Nonempty` is trivially true (const functor / zero nat-trans) but the sorry is a *deliberate flag* that `Div_{C/k}` / the Abel map await their real definitions (see `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` §sorry-closure-order). Papering over them manufactures false progress and removes a real "A.2.b not done" signal. Leave them.

- **The cone's cohomological inputs are not yet available: T2/`AJC.fbc` is still open** (3 leaf sorries in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` — L196 `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits`, L1646/L1712 the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields). `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH) is the intended $\chi$-openness engine for `QuotScheme`'s base-change chain (L596/620/1065/1227/1278/1328), so that chain cannot be discharged from existing infrastructure until T2 lands. **Do not re-open T2's exclusive `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange*` / `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` files** (out of T12 scope).
